context: TaskFlow needs relational storage for workspaces, users, tasks, comments. 20 workspaces pilot, budget-sensitive, one developer, future SaaS phase.

variants.name: Supabase (managed Postgres)
variants.y_statement: In the context of a 20-workspace pilot, facing one-dev maintainability, use Supabase managed Postgres and reject self-hosting, to achieve zero-ops + bundled Auth/Storage, accepting vendor-specific surface.
good: Good, because bundled Auth + Storage + RLS in one project [VERIFIED via supabase.com/docs]
Good, because managed backups + zero-ops for a solo dev
neutral: Neutral, because Postgres-the-DB is portable; only Supabase-specific APIs lock in
bad: Bad, because Pro tier $25/mo floor [UNVERIFIED — knowledge cutoff Jan 2026]
failure_modes: Crash mid-migration → use transactional migrations (idempotent); Supabase runs each in a tx
Vendor outage → managed SLA only; no self-host fallback without rewrite
variants.when_to_choose: Solo dev, wants bundled auth, future SaaS dashboard.
variants.when_not_to_choose: Hard requirement for self-hosted DB or air-gapped deploy.
variants.confirmation: Day-7: restore-to-staging dry-run verifies managed backup works.

variants.name: Neon (serverless Postgres)
variants.y_statement: In the context of the same pilot, facing portability preference, use Neon serverless Postgres and reject bundling, to achieve standard Postgres + scale-to-zero, accepting separate Auth/Storage choices.
good: Good, because pure Postgres, zero vendor lock beyond connection string
Good, because scale-to-zero pricing suits low pilot traffic [UNVERIFIED — knowledge cutoff]
neutral: Neutral, because Auth + Storage chosen separately (more wiring)
bad: Bad, because cold-start on scale-to-zero adds first-query latency
failure_modes: Cold-start timeout under burst → keep a warm connection / disable scale-to-zero
Crash mid-migration → same transactional-migration mitigation as any Postgres
variants.when_to_choose: Want maximum portability, comfortable wiring Auth/Storage yourself.
variants.confirmation: Load test 100 RPS for 1h, p99 < 200ms after warm-up.

variants.name: Self-hosted Postgres (VPS)
variants.y_statement: In the context of the pilot, facing full-control preference, run Postgres on a VPS and reject managed services, to achieve zero vendor lock + lowest cost, accepting full ops burden.
good: Good, because no vendor lock at all, cheapest raw compute
neutral: Neutral, because requires a VPS you already manage
bad: Bad, because backups/patching/HA are all your responsibility — heavy for solo dev
failure_modes: Disk full / no backup → data loss; you own backup cron + monitoring
Crash recovery → you own WAL archiving + restore runbook
variants.when_to_choose: You already run VPS infra and need air-gapped/full control.
variants.when_not_to_choose: Solo dev who values time over raw cost — ops burden too high.
variants.confirmation: Documented restore-from-backup runbook executed successfully once.
